Oratory |Location = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Suita City|Q653510}}{{jalink|Tarumi Town (Suita City)|Q136088206|Tarumi Town}}1-24-6 |Position = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Toyoshiro Irihiko no Mikoto|Q11633375}} |Divinity = |Company status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|Meishin University}})
Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Foundation = ({{jalink|Newly selected surname list|Q1045362|surname list}}) {{jalink|Emperor Kotoku|Q317980}} years ({{jalink|645 years|Q31040}}-{{jalink|654 years|Q31109}}) |Main shrine = {{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|October 21st|Q2960}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tarumi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), sandou-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sando and {{jalink|Senri Hills|Q11406845}}}}]] '''Tarumi Shrine'' (Tarumi Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Suita City|Q653510}}{{jalink|Tarumi Town (Suita City)|Q136088206|Tarumi Town}}. It is {{jalink|Shikiuchi}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|Old shrine rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}. Currently {{jalink|Independent Shrine}} which does not belong to {{jalink|Shinja Honcho|Q908077}}Osaka Prefectural Shrine and Shrine Agency website [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai2shibu/suita-city/suita.html]. ===Enshrined God=== The following three deities are enshrined. . ; Main enshrined deity :* {{jalink|Toyoki Irihiko no Mikoto|Q11633375}} - 10th {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} prince. ; Aidenjin :* {{jalink|Okuninushi|Q276944|Oonamuchi no Mikoto}} :* {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikona no Mikoto}} In "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}, which was established in 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), there is one enshrined deity. In the special ``Engishiki'' festival, there will be one troupe for the prayer rain god festival and the Meishin festival, but there will be two za for the Yasojima festival. === About the enshrined deity === ``Tarumi'' in the company name of Tarumi Shrine means ``water flowing down a cliff'' {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1977}}. {{jalink|Senri Hills|Q11406845}}The precincts located at the southern end are topographically located where spring water comes from the hills, and Tarumi-jin, who is enshrined here, has been worshiped as the god of water since ancient times {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikiuchi-sha)|1977}}. As mentioned below, in the literature, ``{{jalink|Newly Selected Names and Names Roku|Q1045362}}'' records the history of our company related to the water of ``Tarumi Oka'', and also {{jalink| Rokukokushi|Q1151168|Kokokushi}} records that the imperial court made offerings to the shrine many times to pray for rain {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1977}}. {{jalink|Early Modern Period|Q5308718}}'s ``{{jalink|Settsu Meisho Zue|Q11498549}}'' and ``Settsushi'' also contain descriptions of the spring water in this area, and in particular, ``Settsu Famous Places Zue'' describes how it was worshiped as a spiritual spring {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}}.In addition, in the Tarumi area, the ``Tarumi clan'' (Tarumi Uji, Tarumi ko) lived as an ancient clan, and it is known that they were involved in the rituals at Tarumi Shrine. Regarding this Tarumi clan, "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" by Ukyo Emperor In Tarumi Kojo, he was named as a descendant of Kamo no Shinchi-no-Mikoto (the fourth-generation grandchild of Toyoki Irihiko-no-Mikoto), and in his dispensation, he was given the surname of ``Tarumi-ko'' at the time of Arima no Kimi (the sixth-generation grandson of Toyoki Irihiko-no-Mikoto), and the festival was held at Tarumi Shrine. He is said to have been in charge of the shrine. Toyoki Irihiko-no-Mikoto, who is considered a personal deity at the current Tarumi Shrine, is the distant ancestor of the Tarumi clan. One theory is that before the involvement of the Tarumi clan, the shrine had been enshrined as a simple shrine dedicated to the god of water, but it is also speculated that due to the involvement of the Tarumi clan, Toyogi Irihiko no Mikoto was appointed as the personal deity {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. In addition, in the "Newly Selected Names and Names and Records", there is also a record of the history of Tarumi whose ancestor was Irihiko Toyoshiro no Mikoto by Sakyo-no-no-no-Ko.{{Sfn|Tarumi clan (Ancient clan names dictionary)|2010}}{{Sfn|Tarumi (Ancient clan dictionary)|2015}}.In the early modern "Settsu Famous Places Zue," the deity enshrined at the time was Toyogi Irihiko-no-Mikoto, and another theory states that the deity enshrined was {{jalink|Hitachikuni|Q1055353}} Yakushi Shrine (currently {{jalink|Oarai Isosaki Shrine) |Q11437230}}), and there is also a theory that they are Ōki Takamiko and Sukunahikona no Mikoto {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}, and the current Tarumi Shrine enshrines all three of these deities. Also, {{jalink|Nobutomo Ban|Q4346747}} writes in ``{{jalink|Nihonshoki|Q813935}}'' in ``{{jalink|Shinmeichō Kōshōdoshiro|Q11588823}}''. {jalink|Empress Jingu|Q232803}} The theory is that the enshrined deity is {{jalink|Yoriami Otarimi|Q24899491}}, which appears in the {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1977}}. === History === === Founding === The date of its construction is unknown {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1977}}. "{{jalink|Newly selected family name list|Q1045362}}" Ukyo no Betsu According to Tarumi Kojo, the time of {{jalink|Emperor Kotoku|Q317980}} ({{jalink|645 years|Q31040}}-{{jalink |654|Q31109}}), there was a drought, but Prince Arima (grandchild of Toyogi Irihiko no Mikoto VI) traveled from Tarumi-oka to Takahi to visit the Emperor's Palace ({{jalink|difficult). It is said that because of his accomplishments, Prince Arima was given the surname ``Ko Tarumi'' and was put in charge of Tarumi Shrine. Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}}{{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}{{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. It is located in the center of the Tarumi ruins and Tarumi Minami ruins, which are thought to be the core settlements of the Yayoi period. === Outline === According to the ``{{jalink|Shinshōkakuchofusho|Q11502312}}'' {{jalink|Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|Daido}} 1st year ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}), the ``Sumiyoshi Tarumijin'' at that time was {{jalink|Kobe (Private)|Q11589711|Kobe (Kanbe)}} 22 houses are allocated, of which 2 houses are {{jalink|Tenpyoji|Q1346307}}3 years ({{jalink|759 years|Q30532}}) December 29th mark. From nk|Settsu Province|Q864163}}, one house was filled from {{jalink|Bicchu|Q1047514}} with the September 7th mark of the first year of {{jalink|Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181}} ({{jalink|765|Q31216}}). Since the name of the god is prefixed with "Sumiyoshi" in this entry, there is a theory that suggests that it was closely related to {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949}} around this time. . In {{jalink|Rokukokushi|Q1151168|Kokushi}}, there are articles about prayer offerings like the following {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}.:* {{jalink|Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jowa}}3 ({{jalink|836|Q24957}}) June 6, {{jalink|Matsuo Taisha|Q710302|Matsuo}}, { Prayers for rain at shrines such as {jalink|Kamo Miso Shrine|Q701620|Kamo Miso}}, {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|Sumiyoshi}}, and ``Tarumi''Shoku Nihon Koki, June 6th, 836, article (see {{Harvnb|Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection}}). . :* On August 28, 1987 ({{jalink|838|Q23838}}), prayers for hail were made to famous deities such as Kamo and upper, Matsuo, Otokuni, ``Tarumi'', and Sumiyoshi Shoku Nihon Koki, August 28th, 838, article (see {{Harvnb|Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection}}). . :* April 29, 7th year of Jowa ({{jalink|840 years|Q29936}}), Matsuo, Kamo, Otokuni, {{jalink|Kibune Shrine|Q276779 |Kibune}}, {{jalink|Nyukawakami Shrine|Q246370|Nyukawakami Amashi}}, '''Tarumi'''' pray for sweet rain and wind protectionShoku Nihon Koki, April 29th, 840, article (see {{Harvnb|Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection}}). .:* April 29, 841 ({{jalink|841|Q24870}}): Prayers to Matsuo, Kamo, Otokuni, Kifune, '''Tarumi'', Sumiyoshi, and Amashi for protection against rain and wind disasters Shoku Nihon Koki, April 29th, 841, Article (Refer to {{Harvnb|Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection}}). . :* On March 15, 1984 ({{jalink|842|Q24730}}), prayers for sweet rain were sent to shrines such as Kifune, Sumiyoshi, ``Tarumi'', and Nyukawakami Shoku Nihon Koki, March 15th, 842, article (see {{Harvnb|Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection}}). . :* {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} On September 8th, 1999 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), prayers were made for wind and rain to dozens of gods, including '''Tarumi no Kami'' "Nihon Sandai Jiroku" September 8, 859, article (see {{Harvnb|Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection}}). .:* {{jalink|Genkei|Q747451}} June 14th ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}), {{jalink|Iwashimizu Hachimangu Shrine|Q710 098|Iwashimizu Hachiman Daibosatsugu Shrine}}・Kamo Goso・{{jalink|Kamobetsu Thunder Shrine|Q700448|Bettetsu Thunder}}・Matsuo・Inari・{{jalink|Kijimaza Amaterasu Mitama Shrine |Q11518370|Kijima}}・Otokuni・{{jalink|Oyora Shrine|Q11432381|Oyora}}・'''Tarumi''''・{{jalink|Hirota Shrine| Q1466105|Hirota}}, {{jalink|Ikuta Shrine|Q710086|Ikuta}}, {{jalink|Nagata Shrine|Q661395}} to pray for sweet rain "Nihon Sandai Jiroku" June 14th, 877 (see {{Harvnb|Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection}}). . :* On July 15, 3rd year of {{jalink|Owa|Q1324768}} ({{jalink|963|Q23562}}), dozens of shrines, including '''Tarumisha'', prayed for rain. ``Kokushi Taikei Volume 5''] 459 frames). .In addition to the above, in Leap September, 841, there was an article about the investiture of ``Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Rank, Eighth Grade,'' to Tarumi, and in January, 859. In the group="Hara" name="New Year's Day of the First Year of Jogan"/>, you can see an article about promotion to Junior Fourth Rank Lower {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}.{{jalink|Extension (era name)|Q1327274|Extension}} 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}} Settsu Province {{jalink|Toshima County (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11633558|Toshima District}} "Tarumi Shrine Meishin University It is listed in {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} as the ``Monthly Niiname Shrine,'' and it is also stated that it is entrusted to the Imperial Court's {{jalink|Monthly Festival|Q11516161}} and {{jalink|Niinamatsuri|Q11501518}} {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Also, in the special ``Engishiki'' festival, Tarumi is mentioned among the gods who receive medals at the {{jalink|Yasoshima Festival|Q24870749|Yasoshima God Festival}}, Prayer God Festival, and Meishin Festival {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Among the place names that appear in the {{jalink|Japanese Ruijusho|Q2702542|Japanese Namesho}} from the middle of the {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, this place is identified as Toshima-go, Toshima District.{{jalink|Secret (era name)|Q1194464|Security}} In the first year of the year ({{jalink|1120|Q19542}}), the "Settsu Province Tax Book Draft" describes Kobe, the god of Tarumi, as "Tarumi Kobe Ichie". In addition to the "Annual Tribute Order for Jingikan Shrines" in the first year of {{jalink|Eiman|Q1193868}} ({{jalink|1165|Q19676}}) and the fifth year of {{jalink|Bunji|Q1194415}} ({{jalink|1189|Q19735}}) Kasugasha Ryo Tarumi Nishimaki Enokizaka Goda Hatatoricho, {{jalink|Sadaharu|Q1146153}}5 ({{jalink|1366|Q6194 }})'s ``Kasugasha Ryō Tarumi Nishimaki Enokizakagouchi Toho Catalog'' also mentions Tarumi Shrine {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The changes during the {{jalink|Middle Ages|Q12554}} period are not clear because there are almost no descriptions in historical materials{{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. According to the shrine's legend, it declined due to military fire {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, {{jalink|Tenwa (Japan)|Q1145289|Tenwa}} In the 3rd year ({{jalink|1683|Q7651}}), the shrine building was rebuilt.After {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), {{jalink|Modern company rating system|Q712534}} It was listed as a {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}, and in February 1907 (Meiji 40|1907|Q2048}}, it was designated as a Shinto shrine). Later, in {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}49 ({{jalink|1974|Q2478}}), the shrine building you see today was rebuilt. === Modern === {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}In April 2013, a briefing session was held for nearby residents about the plan to demolish Tarumi Shrine's "Chinju no Mori" to develop condominiums.Yomiuri Shimbun (Osaka Morning Edition), December 18, 2013, p. . Parishioners who learned about the condominium development at an information session formed the Tarumi Forest Protection Association and began opposing the project. Around 100 banners were erected around the shrine protesting against the construction of condominiums, posters opposing the construction were put up, and approximately 13,000 people submitted signatures opposing Suita City, which had granted the development permit. They also discovered that construction of condominiums would require roads on land owned by a shrine, and refused construction vehicles access, forcing the development to be halted. Asahi Shimbun (Osaka morning edition), January 11, 2014, p. 33. . {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} In February 2015, a condominium developer approached the shrine about purchasing the planned land, and in November of the same year, the shrine collected donations and purchased the land, and a contract was concluded, thus preserving the ``Chinju-no-Mori.'' According to Itoya, director of the NPO Shaso Gakkai, even if compromises such as revising plans are sometimes passed, it is rare for condominium development to stop. . === God level === * {{jalink|Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jowa}} 8th year ({{jalink|841|Q24870}}) Leap September 2nd, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Rank, Eighth Grade ("{{jalink|Shoku Nippon Koki|Q1041301}}") - Written as "Tarumi no Kami" Article on the 2nd day of Leap September of the 8th year of the Jowa era (841) in "Shoku Nihon Koki" (Refer to {{Harvnb|Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection}}). . * {{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} January 27, 3rd year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), from the upper fifth rank to the lower junior fourth rank ("{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}") - Written as "Tarumi no Kami" New Year's Day 27th, 1st year of Jogan (859) in "Nihon Sandai Jiroku" (Refer to {{Harvnb|Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection}}). . === Precincts === File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), shaden.jpg|Shrine building
{{small|The worship hall is in the foreground, and the main shrine is in the back right. }} File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), sandou-2.jpg|The approach to the shrine grounds File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), sandou-1.jpg|Sando File:Otaki-bridge Otaki Bridge Tarumi Shrine.jpg|Otaki Bridge File:Tarumi Shrine Great Shrine and Fudosha.jpg|Great Shrine and Fudosha File:Tarumi Shrine grounds soil improvement monument.jpg|Soil improvement monument File:Tarumi Shrine approach Ebisusha.jpg|Ebisusha === Setsusha === The following shrines are enshrined as shrines within the precincts and shrines outside the precincts. *Suesha ** Ebisu Shrine - Deities enshrined: Kanayamahiko no Mikoto, Kanayamahime no Mikoto, Toyoukehime no Mikoto, Kotosushi no Mikoto ** Haredo Shrine - Deities enshrined: Ibukito no Mikoto, Seoritsu Hime no Mikoto, Hasakai Tsu Hime no Mikoto, Hasasa Sura Hime no Mikoto ** Kotaisha - Deities enshrined: Isanaki no Mikoto, Isanami no Mikoto, Amaterasu Omikami ** Kusunosha Shrine - Deities enshrined: Owatatsumi no Mikoto, Ichikishimahimekami * Otabisho ** Kusunoki Shrine - Enshrined deity: Owatatsumi no Mikoto, Ichikishimahime no Kami * Others ** Fudosha File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinuchisha === Festival === The following festivals are held at Tarumi Shrine each year. * Monthly Festival/Monthly Festival (1st and 15th of every month) * Fudosha Goma (8th of every month) * Saitan Festival (January 1st) * Tarumi Ebisu (January 10th) * Donto Festival (January 15th) *Setsubun Festival (February 3rd) * Yoimiya (Otabisho Festival) (May 20th) * Spring Festival (May 21st) * Summer Purification (June 30th) * Yoimiya (Otabisho Festival) (October 20th) * Autumn Festival (October 21st) - ``Regular Festival''. * Great Purification (December 31st) === Appeared works === * 1 poem from volume 8 of "{{jalink|Man'yoshu|Q834321}}" (Man'yoshu has three poems in which Tarumi is read, but there are some theories that it is a proper noun and some that it is a common noun) {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}}{{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}New Edition of Japanese Classical Literature Complete Works 7 Man'yoshu (2), Shogakukan, 2004 (Japan Knowledge Edition), p. 293. Ichinosuke Takagi et al.'s notes, "Japanese Classical Literature System, Manyoshu 2", Iwanami Shoten, 1959, P.283 {{Cquote|{{Small|Spring poem
Prince Shiki's poem}}
　Atop the stone-running ``Tarumi'''', you might be able to enjoy the budding spring of the rustle |20px||{{jalink|Prince Shiki|Q11072254}}, "{{jalink|Man'yoshu|Q834321}}" Volume 8 No. 1418[http://infux03.inf.edu.yamaguchi-u.ac.jp/~manyou/ver2_2/manyou_kekka2.php?kekka=08/1418 08/1418] (Yamaguchi University "Manyoushu Search System"). {{Sfn|Shinto/Shrine Historical Materials Collection}}}} === Local information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Suita City|Q653510}}{{jalink|Tarumi Town (Suita City)|Q136088206|Tarumi Town}}1-24-6 '''Access''' Kyuko Railway Namboku Line|Q11401365|Namboku Line}} {{jalink|Esaka Station|Q691770}} (about 20 minutes walk) * bus ** {{jalink|Hankyu Bus|Q11656745}} Suita Line 84/86 Tarumi stop (5 minutes walk) Please note that the bus from Esaka does not stop because this is a circular section. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} '''Original text''' {{reflist|2|group="hara"}} '''Source''' {{reflist|2}} === Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Foundation = ({{jalink|Newly selected surname list|Q1045362|surname list}}) {{jalink|Emperor Kotoku|Q317980}} years ({{jalink|645 years|Q31040}}-{{jalink|654 years|Q31109}}) |Main shrine = {{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|October 21st|Q2960}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tarumi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), sandou-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sando and {{jalink|Senri Hills|Q11406845}}}}]] '''Tarumi Shrine'' (Tarumi Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Suita City|Q653510}}{{jalink|Tarumi Town (Suita City)|Q136088206|Tarumi Town}}. It is {{jalink|Shikiuchi}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|Old shrine rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}. Currently {{jalink|Independent Shrine}} which does not belong to {{jalink|Shinja Honcho|Q908077}}Osaka Prefectural Shrine and Shrine Agency website [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai2shibu/suita-city/suita.html]. ===Enshrined God=== The following three deities are enshrined. . ; Main enshrined deity :* {{jalink|Toyoki Irihiko no Mikoto|Q11633375}} - 10th {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} prince. ; Aidenjin :* {{jalink|Okuninushi|Q276944|Oonamuchi no Mikoto}} :* {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikona no Mikoto}} In "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}, which was established in 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), there is one enshrined deity. In the special ``Engishiki'' festival, there will be one troupe for the prayer rain god festival and the Meishin festival, but there will be two za for the Yasojima festival. === About the enshrined deity === ``Tarumi'' in the company name of Tarumi Shrine means ``water flowing down a cliff'' {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1977}}. {{jalink|Senri Hills|Q11406845}}The precincts located at the southern end are topographically located where spring water comes from the hills, and Tarumi-jin, who is enshrined here, has been worshiped as the god of water since ancient times {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikiuchi-sha)|1977}}. As mentioned below, in the literature, ``{{jalink|Newly Selected Names and Names Roku|Q1045362}}'' records the history of our company related to the water of ``Tarumi Oka'', and also {{jalink| Rokukokushi|Q1151168|Kokokushi}} records that the imperial court made offerings to the shrine many times to pray for rain {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1977}}. {{jalink|Early Modern Period|Q5308718}}'s ``{{jalink|Settsu Meisho Zue|Q11498549}}'' and ``Settsushi'' also contain descriptions of the spring water in this area, and in particular, ``Settsu Famous Places Zue'' describes how it was worshiped as a spiritual spring {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}}.In addition, in the Tarumi area, the ``Tarumi clan'' (Tarumi Uji, Tarumi ko) lived as an ancient clan, and it is known that they were involved in the rituals at Tarumi Shrine. Regarding this Tarumi clan, "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" by Ukyo Emperor In Tarumi Kojo, he was named as a descendant of Kamo no Shinchi-no-Mikoto (the fourth-generation grandchild of Toyoki Irihiko-no-Mikoto), and in his dispensation, he was given the surname of ``Tarumi-ko'' at the time of Arima no Kimi (the sixth-generation grandson of Toyoki Irihiko-no-Mikoto), and the festival was held at Tarumi Shrine. He is said to have been in charge of the shrine. Toyoki Irihiko-no-Mikoto, who is considered a personal deity at the current Tarumi Shrine, is the distant ancestor of the Tarumi clan. One theory is that before the involvement of the Tarumi clan, the shrine had been enshrined as a simple shrine dedicated to the god of water, but it is also speculated that due to the involvement of the Tarumi clan, Toyogi Irihiko no Mikoto was appointed as the personal deity {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. In addition, in the "Newly Selected Names and Names and Records", there is also a record of the history of Tarumi whose ancestor was Irihiko Toyoshiro no Mikoto by Sakyo-no-no-no-Ko.{{Sfn|Tarumi clan (Ancient clan names dictionary)|2010}}{{Sfn|Tarumi (Ancient clan dictionary)|2015}}.In the early modern "Settsu Famous Places Zue," the deity enshrined at the time was Toyogi Irihiko-no-Mikoto, and another theory states that the deity enshrined was {{jalink|Hitachikuni|Q1055353}} Yakushi Shrine (currently {{jalink|Oarai Isosaki Shrine) |Q11437230}}), and there is also a theory that they are Ōki Takamiko and Sukunahikona no Mikoto {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}, and the current Tarumi Shrine enshrines all three of these deities. Also, {{jalink|Nobutomo Ban|Q4346747}} writes in ``{{jalink|Nihonshoki|Q813935}}'' in ``{{jalink|Shinmeichō Kōshōdoshiro|Q11588823}}''. {jalink|Empress Jingu|Q232803}} The theory is that the enshrined deity is {{jalink|Yoriami Otarimi|Q24899491}}, which appears in the {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1977}}. === History === === Founding === The date of its construction is unknown {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1977}}. "{{jalink|Newly selected family name list|Q1045362}}" Ukyo no Betsu According to Tarumi Kojo, the time of {{jalink|Emperor Kotoku|Q317980}} ({{jalink|645 years|Q31040}}-{{jalink |654|Q31109}}), there was a drought, but Prince Arima (grandchild of Toyogi Irihiko no Mikoto VI) traveled from Tarumi-oka to Takahi to visit the Emperor's Palace ({{jalink|difficult). It is said that because of his accomplishments, Prince Arima was given the surname ``Ko Tarumi'' and was put in charge of Tarumi Shrine. Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}}{{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}{{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. It is located in the center of the Tarumi ruins and Tarumi Minami ruins, which are thought to be the core settlements of the Yayoi period. === Outline === According to the ``{{jalink|Shinshōkakuchofusho|Q11502312}}'' {{jalink|Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|Daido}} 1st year ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}), the ``Sumiyoshi Tarumijin'' at that time was {{jalink|Kobe (Private)|Q11589711|Kobe (Kanbe)}} 22 houses are allocated, of which 2 houses are {{jalink|Tenpyoji|Q1346307}}3 years ({{jalink|759 years|Q30532}}) December 29th mark. From nk|Settsu Province|Q864163}}, one house was filled from {{jalink|Bicchu|Q1047514}} with the September 7th mark of the first year of {{jalink|Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181}} ({{jalink|765|Q31216}}). Since the name of the god is prefixed with "Sumiyoshi" in this entry, there is a theory that suggests that it was closely related to {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949}} around this time. . In the group="Hara" name="New Year's Day of the First Year of Jogan"/>, you can see an article about promotion to Junior Fourth Rank Lower {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}.{{jalink|Extension (era name)|Q1327274|Extension}} 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}} Settsu Province {{jalink|Toshima County (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11633558|Toshima District}} "Tarumi Shrine Meishin University It is listed in {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} as the ``Monthly Niiname Shrine,'' and it is also stated that it is entrusted to the Imperial Court's {{jalink|Monthly Festival|Q11516161}} and {{jalink|Niinamatsuri|Q11501518}} {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Also, in the special ``Engishiki'' festival, Tarumi is mentioned among the gods who receive medals at the {{jalink|Yasoshima Festival|Q24870749|Yasoshima God Festival}}, Prayer God Festival, and Meishin Festival {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Among the place names that appear in the {{jalink|Japanese Ruijusho|Q2702542|Japanese Namesho}} from the middle of the {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, this place is identified as Toshima-go, Toshima District.{{jalink|Secret (era name)|Q1194464|Security}} In the first year of the year ({{jalink|1120|Q19542}}), the "Settsu Province Tax Book Draft" describes Kobe, the god of Tarumi, as "Tarumi Kobe Ichie". In addition to the "Annual Tribute Order for Jingikan Shrines" in the first year of {{jalink|Eiman|Q1193868}} ({{jalink|1165|Q19676}}) and the fifth year of {{jalink|Bunji|Q1194415}} ({{jalink|1189|Q19735}}) Kasugasha Ryo Tarumi Nishimaki Enokizaka Goda Hatatoricho, {{jalink|Sadaharu|Q1146153}}5 ({{jalink|1366|Q6194 }})'s ``Kasugasha Ryō Tarumi Nishimaki Enokizakagouchi Toho Catalog'' also mentions Tarumi Shrine {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The changes during the {{jalink|Middle Ages|Q12554}} period are not clear because there are almost no descriptions in historical materials{{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. According to the shrine's legend, it declined due to military fire {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, {{jalink|Tenwa (Japan)|Q1145289|Tenwa}} In the 3rd year ({{jalink|1683|Q7651}}), the shrine building was rebuilt.After {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), {{jalink|Modern company rating system|Q712534}} It was listed as a {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}, and in February 1907 (Meiji 40|1907|Q2048}}, it was designated as a Shinto shrine). Later, in {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}49 ({{jalink|1974|Q2478}}), the shrine building you see today was rebuilt. === Modern === {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}In April 2013, a briefing session was held for nearby residents about the plan to demolish Tarumi Shrine's "Chinju no Mori" to develop condominiums.Yomiuri Shimbun (Osaka Morning Edition), December 18, 2013, p. . Parishioners who learned about the condominium development at an information session formed the Tarumi Forest Protection Association and began opposing the project. Around 100 banners were erected around the shrine protesting against the construction of condominiums, posters opposing the construction were put up, and approximately 13,000 people submitted signatures opposing Suita City, which had granted the development permit. They also discovered that construction of condominiums would require roads on land owned by a shrine, and refused construction vehicles access, forcing the development to be halted. Asahi Shimbun (Osaka morning edition), January 11, 2014, p. 33. . {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} In February 2015, a condominium developer approached the shrine about purchasing the planned land, and in November of the same year, the shrine collected donations and purchased the land, and a contract was concluded, thus preserving the ``Chinju-no-Mori.'' According to Itoya, director of the NPO Shaso Gakkai, even if compromises such as revising plans are sometimes passed, it is rare for condominium development to stop. . === God level === * {{jalink|Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jowa}} 8th year ({{jalink|841|Q24870}}) Leap September 2nd, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Rank, Eighth Grade ("{{jalink|Shoku Nippon Koki|Q1041301}}") - Written as "Tarumi no Kami" Article on the 2nd day of Leap September of the 8th year of the Jowa era (841) in "Shoku Nihon Koki" (Refer to {{Harvnb|Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection}}). . * {{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} January 27, 3rd year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), from the upper fifth rank to the lower junior fourth rank ("{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}") - Written as "Tarumi no Kami" New Year's Day 27th, 1st year of Jogan (859) in "Nihon Sandai Jiroku" (Refer to {{Harvnb|Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection}}). . === Precincts === File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), shaden.jpg|Shrine building

Deities enshrined: Kanayamahiko no Mikoto, Kanayamahime no Mikoto, Toyoukehime no Mikoto, Kotosushi no Mikoto ** Haredo Shrine - Deities enshrined: Ibukito no Mikoto, Seoritsu Hime no Mikoto, Hasakai Tsu Hime no Mikoto, Hasasa Sura Hime no Mikoto ** Kotaisha - Deities enshrined: Isanaki no Mikoto, Isanami no Mikoto, Amaterasu Omikami ** Kusunosha Shrine - Deities enshrined: Owatatsumi no Mikoto, Ichikishimahimekami * Otabisho ** Kusunoki Shrine - Enshrined deity: Owatatsumi no Mikoto, Ichikishimahime no Kami * Others ** Fudosha File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinuchisha === Festival === The following festivals are held at Tarumi Shrine each year. * Monthly Festival/Monthly Festival (1st and 15th of every month) * Fudosha Goma (8th of every month) * Saitan Festival (January 1st) * Tarumi Ebisu (January 10th) * Donto Festival (January 15th) *Setsubun Festival (February 3rd) * Yoimiya (Otabisho Festival) (May 20th) * Spring Festival (May 21st) * Summer Purification (June 30th) * Yoimiya (Otabisho Festival) (October 20th) * Autumn Festival (October 21st) - Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Foundation = ({{jalink|Newly selected surname list|Q1045362|surname list}}) {{jalink|Emperor Kotoku|Q317980}} years ({{jalink|645 years|Q31040}}-{{jalink|654 years|Q31109}}) |Main shrine = {{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|October 21st|Q2960}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tarumi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), sandou-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sando and {{jalink|Senri Hills|Q11406845}}}}]] '''Tarumi Shrine'' (Tarumi Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Suita City|Q653510}}{{jalink|Tarumi Town (Suita City)|Q136088206|Tarumi Town}}. It is {{jalink|Shikiuchi}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|Old shrine rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}. Currently {{jalink|Independent Shrine}} which does not belong to {{jalink|Shinja Honcho|Q908077}}Osaka Prefectural Shrine and Shrine Agency website [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai2shibu/suita-city/suita.html]. ===Enshrined God=== The following three deities are enshrined. . ; Main enshrined deity :* {{jalink|Toyoki Irihiko no Mikoto|Q11633375}} - 10th {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} prince. ; Aidenjin :* {{jalink|Okuninushi|Q276944|Oonamuchi no Mikoto}} :* {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikona no Mikoto}} In "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}, which was established in 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), there is one enshrined deity. In the special ``Engishiki'' festival, there will be one troupe for the prayer rain god festival and the Meishin festival, but there will be two za for the Yasojima festival. === About the enshrined deity === ``Tarumi'' in the company name of Tarumi Shrine means ``water flowing down a cliff'' {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1977}}. {{jalink|Senri Hills|Q11406845}}The precincts located at the southern end are topographically located where spring water comes from the hills, and Tarumi-jin, who is enshrined here, has been worshiped as the god of water since ancient times {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikiuchi-sha)|1977}}. As mentioned below, in the literature, ``{{jalink|Newly Selected Names and Names Roku|Q1045362}}'' records the history of our company related to the water of ``Tarumi Oka'', and also {{jalink| Rokukokushi|Q1151168|Kokokushi}} records that the imperial court made offerings to the shrine many times to pray for rain {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1977}}. {{jalink|Early Modern Period|Q5308718}}'s ``{{jalink|Settsu Meisho Zue|Q11498549}}'' and ``Settsushi'' also contain descriptions of the spring water in this area, and in particular, ``Settsu Famous Places Zue'' describes how it was worshiped as a spiritual spring {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}}.In addition, in the Tarumi area, the ``Tarumi clan'' (Tarumi Uji, Tarumi ko) lived as an ancient clan, and it is known that they were involved in the rituals at Tarumi Shrine. Regarding this Tarumi clan, "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" by Ukyo Emperor In Tarumi Kojo, he was named as a descendant of Kamo no Shinchi-no-Mikoto (the fourth-generation grandchild of Toyoki Irihiko-no-Mikoto), and in his dispensation, he was given the surname of ``Tarumi-ko'' at the time of Arima no Kimi (the sixth-generation grandson of Toyoki Irihiko-no-Mikoto), and the festival was held at Tarumi Shrine. He is said to have been in charge of the shrine. Toyoki Irihiko-no-Mikoto, who is considered a personal deity at the current Tarumi Shrine, is the distant ancestor of the Tarumi clan. One theory is that before the involvement of the Tarumi clan, the shrine had been enshrined as a simple shrine dedicated to the god of water, but it is also speculated that due to the involvement of the Tarumi clan, Toyogi Irihiko no Mikoto was appointed as the personal deity {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. In addition, in the "Newly Selected Names and Names and Records", there is also a record of the history of Tarumi whose ancestor was Irihiko Toyoshiro no Mikoto by Sakyo-no-no-no-Ko.{{Sfn|Tarumi clan (Ancient clan names dictionary)|2010}}{{Sfn|Tarumi (Ancient clan dictionary)|2015}}.In the early modern "Settsu Famous Places Zue," the deity enshrined at the time was Toyogi Irihiko-no-Mikoto, and another theory states that the deity enshrined was {{jalink|Hitachikuni|Q1055353}} Yakushi Shrine (currently {{jalink|Oarai Isosaki Shrine) |Q11437230}}), and there is also a theory that they are Ōki Takamiko and Sukunahikona no Mikoto {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}, and the current Tarumi Shrine enshrines all three of these deities. Also, {{jalink|Nobutomo Ban|Q4346747}} writes in ``{{jalink|Nihonshoki|Q813935}}'' in ``{{jalink|Shinmeichō Kōshōdoshiro|Q11588823}}''. {jalink|Empress Jingu|Q232803}} The theory is that the enshrined deity is {{jalink|Yoriami Otarimi|Q24899491}}, which appears in the {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1977}}. === History === === Founding === The date of its construction is unknown {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1977}}. "{{jalink|Newly selected family name list|Q1045362}}" Ukyo no Betsu According to Tarumi Kojo, the time of {{jalink|Emperor Kotoku|Q317980}} ({{jalink|645 years|Q31040}}-{{jalink |654|Q31109}}), there was a drought, but Prince Arima (grandchild of Toyogi Irihiko no Mikoto VI) traveled from Tarumi-oka to Takahi to visit the Emperor's Palace ({{jalink|difficult). It is said that because of his accomplishments, Prince Arima was given the surname ``Ko Tarumi'' and was put in charge of Tarumi Shrine. Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}}{{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}{{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. It is located in the center of the Tarumi ruins and Tarumi Minami ruins, which are thought to be the core settlements of the Yayoi period. === Outline === According to the ``{{jalink|Shinshōkakuchofusho|Q11502312}}'' {{jalink|Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|Daido}} 1st year ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}), the ``Sumiyoshi Tarumijin'' at that time was {{jalink|Kobe (Private)|Q11589711|Kobe (Kanbe)}} 22 houses are allocated, of which 2 houses are {{jalink|Tenpyoji|Q1346307}}3 years ({{jalink|759 years|Q30532}}) December 29th mark. From nk|Settsu Province|Q864163}}, one house was filled from {{jalink|Bicchu|Q1047514}} with the September 7th mark of the first year of {{jalink|Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181}} ({{jalink|765|Q31216}}). Since the name of the god is prefixed with "Sumiyoshi" in this entry, there is a theory that suggests that it was closely related to {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949}} around this time. . In {{jalink|Rokukokushi|Q1151168|Kokushi}}, there are articles about prayer offerings like the following {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}.:* {{jalink|Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jowa}}3 Rank, Eighth Grade,'' to Tarumi, and in January, 859. In the group="Hara" name="New Year's Day of the First Year of Jogan"/>, you can see an article about promotion to Junior Fourth Rank Lower {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}.{{jalink|Extension (era name)|Q1327274|Extension}} 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}} Settsu Province {{jalink|Toshima County (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11633558|Toshima District}} "Tarumi Shrine Meishin University It is listed in {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} as the ``Monthly Niiname Shrine,'' and it is also stated that it is entrusted to the Imperial Court's {{jalink|Monthly Festival|Q11516161}} and {{jalink|Niinamatsuri|Q11501518}} {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Also, in the special ``Engishiki'' festival, Tarumi is mentioned among the gods who receive medals at the {{jalink|Yasoshima Festival|Q24870749|Yasoshima God Festival}}, Prayer God Festival, and Meishin Festival {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Among the place names that appear in the {{jalink|Japanese Ruijusho|Q2702542|Japanese Namesho}} from the middle of the {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, this place is identified as Toshima-go, Toshima District.{{jalink|Secret (era name)|Q1194464|Security}} In the first year of the year ({{jalink|1120|Q19542}}), the "Settsu Province Tax Book Draft" describes Kobe, the god of Tarumi, as "Tarumi Kobe Ichie". In addition to the "Annual Tribute Order for Jingikan Shrines" in the first year of {{jalink|Eiman|Q1193868}} ({{jalink|1165|Q19676}}) and the fifth year of {{jalink|Bunji|Q1194415}} ({{jalink|1189|Q19735}}) Kasugasha Ryo Tarumi Nishimaki Enokizaka Goda Hatatoricho, {{jalink|Sadaharu|Q1146153}}5 ({{jalink|1366|Q6194 }})'s ``Kasugasha Ryō Tarumi Nishimaki Enokizakagouchi Toho Catalog'' also mentions Tarumi Shrine {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The changes during the {{jalink|Middle Ages|Q12554}} period are not clear because there are almost no descriptions in historical materials{{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. According to the shrine's legend, it declined due to military fire {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, {{jalink|Tenwa (Japan)|Q1145289|Tenwa}} In the 3rd year ({{jalink|1683|Q7651}}), the shrine building was rebuilt.After {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), {{jalink|Modern company rating system|Q712534}} It was listed as a {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}, and in February 1907 (Meiji 40|1907|Q2048}}, it was designated as a Shinto shrine). Later, in {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}49 ({{jalink|1974|Q2478}}), the shrine building you see today was rebuilt. === Modern === {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}In April 2013, a briefing session was held for nearby residents about the plan to demolish Tarumi Shrine's "Chinju no Mori" to develop condominiums.Yomiuri Shimbun (Osaka Morning Edition), December 18, 2013, p. . Parishioners who learned about the condominium development at an information session formed the Tarumi Forest Protection Association and began opposing the project. Around 100 banners were erected around the shrine protesting against the construction of condominiums, posters opposing the construction were put up, and approximately 13,000 people submitted signatures opposing Suita City, which had granted the development permit. They also discovered that construction of condominiums would require roads on land owned by a shrine, and refused construction vehicles access, forcing the development to be halted. Asahi Shimbun (Osaka morning edition), January 11, 2014, p. 33. . {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} In February 2015, a condominium developer approached the shrine about purchasing the planned land, and in November of the same year, the shrine collected donations and purchased the land, and a contract was concluded, thus preserving the ``Chinju-no-Mori.'' According to Itoya, director of the NPO Shaso Gakkai, even if compromises such as revising plans are sometimes passed, it is rare for condominium development to stop. . === God level === * {{jalink|Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jowa}} 8th year ({{jalink|841|Q24870}}) Leap September 2nd, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Rank, Eighth Grade ("{{jalink|Shoku Nippon Koki|Q1041301}}") - Written as "Tarumi no Kami" Article on the 2nd day of Leap September of the 8th year of the Jowa era (841) in "Shoku Nihon Koki" (Refer to {{Harvnb|Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection}}). . * {{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} January 27, 3rd year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), from the upper fifth rank to the lower junior fourth rank ("{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}") - Written as "Tarumi no Kami" New Year's Day 27th, 1st year of Jogan (859) in "Nihon Sandai Jiroku" (Refer to {{Harvnb|Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection}}). . === Precincts === File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), shaden.jpg|Shrine building

Deities enshrined: Kanayamahiko no Mikoto, Kanayamahime no Mikoto, Toyoukehime no Mikoto, Kotosushi no Mikoto ** Haredo Shrine - Deities enshrined: Ibukito no Mikoto, Seoritsu Hime no Mikoto, Hasakai Tsu Hime no Mikoto, Hasasa Sura Hime no Mikoto ** Kotaisha - Deities enshrined: Isanaki no Mikoto, Isanami no Mikoto, Amaterasu Omikami ** Kusunosha Shrine - Deities enshrined: Owatatsumi no Mikoto, Ichikishimahimekami * Otabisho ** Kusunoki Shrine - Enshrined deity: Owatatsumi no Mikoto, Ichikishimahime no Kami * Others ** Fudosha File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinuchisha === Festival === The following festivals are held at Tarumi Shrine each year. * Monthly Festival/Monthly Festival (1st and 15th of every month) * Fudosha Goma (8th of every month) * Saitan Festival (January 1st) * Tarumi Ebisu (January 10th) * Donto Festival (January 15th) *Setsubun Festival (February 3rd) * Yoimiya (Otabisho Festival) (May 20th) * Spring Festival (May 21st) * Summer Purification (June 30th) * Yoimiya (Otabisho Festival) (October 20th) * Autumn Festival (October 21st) - Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Foundation = ({{jalink|Newly selected surname list|Q1045362|surname list}}) {{jalink|Emperor Kotoku|Q317980}} years ({{jalink|645 years|Q31040}}-{{jalink|654 years|Q31109}}) |Main shrine = {{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|October 21st|Q2960}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tarumi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), sandou-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sando and {{jalink|Senri Hills|Q11406845}}}}]] '''Tarumi Shrine'' (Tarumi Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Suita City|Q653510}}{{jalink|Tarumi Town (Suita City)|Q136088206|Tarumi Town}}. It is {{jalink|Shikiuchi}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|Old shrine rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}. Currently {{jalink|Independent Shrine}} which does not belong to {{jalink|Shinja Honcho|Q908077}}Osaka Prefectural Shrine and Shrine Agency website [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai2shibu/suita-city/suita.html]. ===Enshrined God=== The following three deities are enshrined. . ; Main enshrined deity :* {{jalink|Toyoki Irihiko no Mikoto|Q11633375}} - 10th {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} prince. ; Aidenjin :* {{jalink|Okuninushi|Q276944|Oonamuchi no Mikoto}} :* {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikona no Mikoto}} In "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}, which was established in 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), there is one enshrined deity. In the special ``Engishiki'' festival, there will be one troupe for the prayer rain god festival and the Meishin festival, but there will be two za for the Yasojima festival. === About the enshrined deity === ``Tarumi'' in the company name of Tarumi Shrine means ``water flowing down a cliff'' {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1977}}. {{jalink|Senri Hills|Q11406845}}The precincts located at the southern end are topographically located where spring water comes from the hills, and Tarumi-jin, who is enshrined here, has been worshiped as the god of water since ancient times {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikiuchi-sha)|1977}}. As mentioned below, in the literature, ``{{jalink|Newly Selected Names and Names Roku|Q1045362}}'' records the history of our company related to the water of ``Tarumi Oka'', and also {{jalink| Rokukokushi|Q1151168|Kokokushi}} records that the imperial court made offerings to the shrine many times to pray for rain {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1977}}. {{jalink|Early Modern Period|Q5308718}}'s ``{{jalink|Settsu Meisho Zue|Q11498549}}'' and ``Settsushi'' also contain descriptions of the spring water in this area, and in particular, ``Settsu Famous Places Zue'' describes how it was worshiped as a spiritual spring {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}}.In addition, in the Tarumi area, the ``Tarumi clan'' (Tarumi Uji, Tarumi ko) lived as an ancient clan, and it is known that they were involved in the rituals at Tarumi Shrine. Regarding this Tarumi clan, "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" by Ukyo Emperor In Tarumi Kojo, he was named as a descendant of Kamo no Shinchi-no-Mikoto (the fourth-generation grandchild of Toyoki Irihiko-no-Mikoto), and in his dispensation, he was given the surname of ``Tarumi-ko'' at the time of Arima no Kimi (the sixth-generation grandson of Toyoki Irihiko-no-Mikoto), and the festival was held at Tarumi Shrine. He is said to have been in charge of the shrine. Toyoki Irihiko-no-Mikoto, who is considered a personal deity at the current Tarumi Shrine, is the distant ancestor of the Tarumi clan. One theory is that before the involvement of the Tarumi clan, the shrine had been enshrined as a simple shrine dedicated to the god of water, but it is also speculated that due to the involvement of the Tarumi clan, Toyogi Irihiko no Mikoto was appointed as the personal deity {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. In addition, in the "Newly Selected Names and Names and Records", there is also a record of the history of Tarumi whose ancestor was Irihiko Toyoshiro no Mikoto by Sakyo-no-no-no-Ko.{{Sfn|Tarumi clan (Ancient clan names dictionary)|2010}}{{Sfn|Tarumi (Ancient clan dictionary)|2015}}.In the early modern "Settsu Famous Places Zue," the deity enshrined at the time was Toyogi Irihiko-no-Mikoto, and another theory states that the deity enshrined was {{jalink|Hitachikuni|Q1055353}} Yakushi Shrine (currently {{jalink|Oarai Isosaki Shrine) |Q11437230}}), and there is also a theory that they are Ōki Takamiko and Sukunahikona no Mikoto {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}, and the current Tarumi Shrine enshrines all three of these deities. Also, {{jalink|Nobutomo Ban|Q4346747}} writes in ``{{jalink|Nihonshoki|Q813935}}'' in ``{{jalink|Shinmeichō Kōshōdoshiro|Q11588823}}''. {jalink|Empress Jingu|Q232803}} The theory is that the enshrined deity is {{jalink|Yoriami Otarimi|Q24899491}}, which appears in the {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1977}}. === History === === Founding === The date of its construction is unknown {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1977}}. "{{jalink|Newly selected family name list|Q1045362}}" Ukyo no Betsu According to Tarumi Kojo, the time of {{jalink|Emperor Kotoku|Q317980}} ({{jalink|645 years|Q31040}}-{{jalink |654|Q31109}}), there was a drought, but Prince Arima (grandchild of Toyogi Irihiko no Mikoto VI) traveled from Tarumi-oka to Takahi to visit the Emperor's Palace ({{jalink|difficult). It is said that because of his accomplishments, Prince Arima was given the surname ``Ko Tarumi'' and was put in charge of Tarumi Shrine. Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}}{{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}{{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. It is located in the center of the Tarumi ruins and Tarumi Minami ruins, which are thought to be the core settlements of the Yayoi period. === Outline === According to the ``{{jalink|Shinshōkakuchofusho|Q11502312}}'' {{jalink|Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|Daido}} 1st year ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}), the ``Sumiyoshi Tarumijin'' at that time was {{jalink|Kobe (Private)|Q11589711|Kobe (Kanbe)}} 22 houses are allocated, of which 2 houses are {{jalink|Tenpyoji|Q1346307}}3 years ({{jalink|759 years|Q30532}}) December 29th mark. From nk|Settsu Province|Q864163}}, one house was filled from {{jalink|Bicchu|Q1047514}} with the September 7th mark of the first year of {{jalink|Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181}} ({{jalink|765|Q31216}}). Since the name of the god is prefixed with "Sumiyoshi" in this entry, there is a theory that suggests that it was closely related to {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949}} around this time. . In {{jalink|Rokukokushi|Q1151168|Kokushi}}, there are articles about prayer offerings like the following {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}.:* {{jalink|Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jowa}}3 Rank, Eighth Grade,'' to Tarumi, and in January, 859. In the group="Hara" name="New Year's Day of the First Year of Jogan"/>, you can see an article about promotion to Junior Fourth Rank Lower {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}.{{jalink|Extension (era name)|Q1327274|Extension}} 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}} Settsu Province {{jalink|Toshima County (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11633558|Toshima District}} "Tarumi Shrine Meishin University It is listed in {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} as the ``Monthly Niiname Shrine,'' and it is also stated that it is entrusted to the Imperial Court's {{jalink|Monthly Festival|Q11516161}} and {{jalink|Niinamatsuri|Q11501518}} {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Also, in the special ``Engishiki'' festival, Tarumi is mentioned among the gods who receive medals at the {{jalink|Yasoshima Festival|Q24870749|Yasoshima God Festival}}, Prayer God Festival, and Meishin Festival {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Among the place names that appear in the {{jalink|Japanese Ruijusho|Q2702542|Japanese Namesho}} from the middle of the {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, this place is identified as Toshima-go, Toshima District.{{jalink|Secret (era name)|Q1194464|Security}} In the first year of the year ({{jalink|1120|Q19542}}), the "Settsu Province Tax Book Draft" describes Kobe, the god of Tarumi, as "Tarumi Kobe Ichie". In addition to the "Annual Tribute Order for Jingikan Shrines" in the first year of {{jalink|Eiman|Q1193868}} ({{jalink|1165|Q19676}}) and the fifth year of {{jalink|Bunji|Q1194415}} ({{jalink|1189|Q19735}}) Kasugasha Ryo Tarumi Nishimaki Enokizaka Goda Hatatoricho, {{jalink|Sadaharu|Q1146153}}5 ({{jalink|1366|Q6194 }})'s ``Kasugasha Ryō Tarumi Nishimaki Enokizakagouchi Toho Catalog'' also mentions Tarumi Shrine {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The changes during the {{jalink|Middle Ages|Q12554}} period are not clear because there are almost no descriptions in historical materials{{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. According to the shrine's legend, it declined due to military fire {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, {{jalink|Tenwa (Japan)|Q1145289|Tenwa}} In the 3rd year ({{jalink|1683|Q7651}}), the shrine building was rebuilt.After {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), {{jalink|Modern company rating system|Q712534}} It was listed as a {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}, and in February 1907 (Meiji 40|1907|Q2048}}, it was designated as a Shinto shrine). Later, in {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}49 ({{jalink|1974|Q2478}}), the shrine building you see today was rebuilt. === Modern === {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}In April 2013, a briefing session was held for nearby residents about the plan to demolish Tarumi Shrine's "Chinju no Mori" to develop condominiums.Yomiuri Shimbun (Osaka Morning Edition), December 18, 2013, p. . Parishioners who learned about the condominium development at an information session formed the Tarumi Forest Protection Association and began opposing the project. Around 100 banners were erected around the shrine protesting against the construction of condominiums, posters opposing the construction were put up, and approximately 13,000 people submitted signatures opposing Suita City, which had granted the development permit. They also discovered that construction of condominiums would require roads on land owned by a shrine, and refused construction vehicles access, forcing the development to be halted. Asahi Shimbun (Osaka morning edition), January 11, 2014, p. 33. . {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} In February 2015, a condominium developer approached the shrine about purchasing the planned land, and in November of the same year, the shrine collected donations and purchased the land, and a contract was concluded, thus preserving the ``Chinju-no-Mori.'' According to Itoya, director of the NPO Shaso Gakkai, even if compromises such as revising plans are sometimes passed, it is rare for condominium development to stop. . === God level === * {{jalink|Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jowa}} 8th year ({{jalink|841|Q24870}}) Leap September 2nd, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Rank, Eighth Grade ("{{jalink|Shoku Nippon Koki|Q1041301}}") - Written as "Tarumi no Kami" Article on the 2nd day of Leap September of the 8th year of the Jowa era (841) in "Shoku Nihon Koki" (Refer to {{Harvnb|Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection}}). . * {{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} January 27, 3rd year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), from the upper fifth rank to the lower junior fourth rank ("{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}") - Written as "Tarumi no Kami" New Year's Day 27th, 1st year of Jogan (859) in "Nihon Sandai Jiroku" (Refer to {{Harvnb|Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection}}). . === Precincts === File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), shaden.jpg|Shrine building
{{small|The worship hall is in the foreground, and the main shrine is in the back right. }} File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), sandou-2.jpg|The approach to the shrine grounds File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), sandou-1.jpg|Sando File:Otaki-bridge Otaki Bridge Tarumi Shrine.jpg|Otaki Bridge File:Tarumi Shrine Great Shrine and Fudosha.jpg|Great Shrine and Fudosha File:Tarumi Shrine grounds soil improvement monument.jpg|Soil improvement monument File:Tarumi Shrine approach Ebisusha.jpg|Ebisusha === Setsusha === The following shrines are enshrined as shrines within the precincts and shrines outside the precincts. *Suesha ** Ebisu Shrine - Deities enshrined: Kanayamahiko no Mikoto, Kanayamahime no Mikoto, Toyoukehime no Mikoto, Kotosushi no Mikoto ** Haredo Shrine - Deities enshrined: Ibukito no Mikoto, Seoritsu Hime no Mikoto, Hasakai Tsu Hime no Mikoto, Hasasa Sura Hime no Mikoto ** Kotaisha - Deities enshrined: Isanaki no Mikoto, Isanami no Mikoto, Amaterasu Omikami ** Kusunosha Shrine - Deities enshrined: Owatatsumi no Mikoto, Ichikishimahimekami * Otabisho ** Kusunoki Shrine - Enshrined deity: Owatatsumi no Mikoto, Ichikishimahime no Kami * Others ** Fudosha File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinuchisha === Festival === The following festivals are held at Tarumi Shrine each year. * Monthly Festival/Monthly Festival (1st and 15th of every month) * Fudosha Goma (8th of every month) * Saitan Festival (January 1st) * Tarumi Ebisu (January 10th) * Donto Festival (January 15th) *Setsubun Festival (February 3rd) * Yoimiya (Otabisho Festival) (May 20th) * Spring Festival (May 21st) * Summer Purification (June 30th) * Yoimiya (Otabisho Festival) (October 20th) * Autumn Festival (October 21st) - ``Regular Festival''. * Great Purification (December 31st) === Appeared works === * 1 poem from volume 8 of "{{jalink|Man'yoshu|Q834321}}" (Man'yoshu has three poems in which Tarumi is read, but there are some theories that it is a proper noun and some that it is a common noun) {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}}{{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}New Edition of Japanese Classical Literature Complete Works 7 Man'yoshu (2), Shogakukan, 2004 (Japan Knowledge Edition), p. 293. It is {{ill||lt=Shikiuchi|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. Currently {{ill||lt=Independent Shrine|UNKNOWN}} which does not belong to {{ill|lt=Shinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077}}Osaka Prefectural Shrine and Shrine Agency website [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai2shibu/suita-city/suita.html]. ===Enshrined God=== The following three deities are enshrined. . ; Main enshrined deity :* {{ill|lt=Toyoki Irihiko no Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375}} - 10th {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} prince. ; Aidenjin :* {{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Oonamuchi no Mikoto}} :* {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona no Mikoto}} In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}}, which was established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), there is one enshrined deity. In the special ``Engishiki'' festival, there will be one troupe for the prayer rain god festival and the Meishin festival, but there will be two za for the Yasojima festival. === About the enshrined deity === ``Tarumi'' in the company name of Tarumi Shrine means ``water flowing down a cliff'' {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1977}}. {{ill|lt=Senri Hills|Q11406845|WD=Q11406845}}The precincts located at the southern end are topographically located where spring water comes from the hills, and Tarumi-jin, who is enshrined here, has been worshiped as the god of water since ancient times {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikiuchi-sha)|1977}}. As mentioned below, in the literature, ``{{ill|lt=Newly Selected Names and Names Roku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}'' records the history of our company related to the water of ``Tarumi Oka'', and also {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Kokokushi}} records that the imperial court made offerings to the shrine many times to pray for rain {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1977}}. {{ill|lt=Early Modern Period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}}'s ``{{ill|lt=Settsu Meisho Zue|Q11498549|WD=Q11498549}}'' and ``Settsushi'' also contain descriptions of the spring water in this area, and in particular, ``Settsu Famous Places Zue'' describes how it was worshiped as a spiritual spring {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}}.In addition, in the Tarumi area, the ``Tarumi clan'' (Tarumi Uji, Tarumi ko) lived as an ancient clan, and it is known that they were involved in the rituals at Tarumi Shrine. Regarding this Tarumi clan, "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" by Ukyo Emperor In Tarumi Kojo, he was named as a descendant of Kamo no Shinchi-no-Mikoto (the fourth-generation grandchild of Toyoki Irihiko-no-Mikoto), and in his dispensation, he was given the surname of ``Tarumi-ko'' at the time of Arima no Kimi (the sixth-generation grandson of Toyoki Irihiko-no-Mikoto), and the festival was held at Tarumi Shrine. He is said to have been in charge of the shrine. Toyoki Irihiko-no-Mikoto, who is considered a personal deity at the current Tarumi Shrine, is the distant ancestor of the Tarumi clan. One theory is that before the involvement of the Tarumi clan, the shrine had been enshrined as a simple shrine dedicated to the god of water, but it is also speculated that due to the involvement of the Tarumi clan, Toyogi Irihiko no Mikoto was appointed as the personal deity {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. In addition, in the "Newly Selected Names and Names and Records", there is also a record of the history of Tarumi whose ancestor was Irihiko Toyoshiro no Mikoto by Sakyo-no-no-no-Ko.{{Sfn|Tarumi clan (Ancient clan names dictionary)|2010}}{{Sfn|Tarumi (Ancient clan dictionary)|2015}}.In the early modern "Settsu Famous Places Zue," the deity enshrined at the time was Toyogi Irihiko-no-Mikoto, and another theory states that the deity enshrined was {{ill|lt=Hitachikuni|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353}} Yakushi Shrine (currently {{ill|lt=Oarai Isosaki Shrine)|Q11437230|WD=Q11437230}}), and there is also a theory that they are Ōki Takamiko and Sukunahikona no Mikoto {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}, and the current Tarumi Shrine enshrines all three of these deities. Also, {{ill|lt=Nobutomo Ban|Q4346747|WD=Q4346747}} writes in ``{{ill|lt=Nihonshoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'' in ``{{ill|lt=Shinmeichō Kōshōdoshiro|Q11588823|WD=Q11588823}}''. {jalink|Empress Jingu|Q232803}} The theory is that the enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Yoriami Otarimi|Q24899491|WD=Q24899491}}, which appears in the {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1977}}. === History === === Founding === The date of its construction is unknown {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1977}}. "{{ill|lt=Newly selected family name list|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}" Ukyo no Betsu According to Tarumi Kojo, the time of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kotoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980}} ({{ill|lt=645 years|Q31040|WD=Q31040}}-{{jalink |654|Q31109}}), there was a drought, but Prince Arima (grandchild of Toyogi Irihiko no Mikoto VI) traveled from Tarumi-oka to Takahi to visit the Emperor's Palace ({{ill|lt=difficult). It is said that because of his accomplishments, Prince Arima was given the surname ``Ko Tarumi'' and was put in charge of Tarumi Shrine. Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|WD=Tarumi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|lt=1977}}{{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}{{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. It is located in the center of the Tarumi ruins and Tarumi Minami ruins, which are thought to be the core settlements of the Yayoi period. === Outline === According to the ``{{ill|lt=Shinshōkakuchofusho|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312}}'' {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}), the ``Sumiyoshi Tarumijin'' at that time was {{ill|lt=Kobe (Private)|Q11589711|WD=Q11589711|lt=Kobe (Kanbe)}} 22 houses are allocated, of which 2 houses are {{ill|lt=Tenpyoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307}}3 years ({{ill|lt=759 years|Q30532|WD=Q30532}}) December 29th mark. From nk|Settsu Province|Q864163}}, one house was filled from {{ill|lt=Bicchu|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514}} with the September 7th mark of the first year of {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181|WD=Q1373181}} ({{ill|lt=765|Q31216|WD=Q31216}}). Since the name of the god is prefixed with "Sumiyoshi" in this entry, there is a theory that suggests that it was closely related to {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949}} around this time. . Also, in the special ``Engishiki'' festival, Tarumi is mentioned among the gods who receive medals at the {{ill|lt=Yasoshima Festival|Q24870749|WD=Q24870749|lt=Yasoshima God Festival}}, Prayer God Festival, and Meishin Festival {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Among the place names that appear in the {{ill|lt=Japanese Ruijusho|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|lt=Japanese Namesho}} from the middle of the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, this place is identified as Toshima-go, Toshima District.{{ill|lt=Secret (era name)|Q1194464|WD=Q1194464|lt=Security}} In the first year of the year ({{ill|lt=1120|Q19542|WD=Q19542}}), the "Settsu Province Tax Book Draft" describes Kobe, the god of Tarumi, as "Tarumi Kobe Ichie". In addition to the "Annual Tribute Order for Jingikan Shrines" in the first year of {{ill|lt=Eiman|Q1193868|WD=Q1193868}} ({{ill|lt=1165|Q19676|WD=Q19676}}) and the fifth year of {{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415}} ({{ill|lt=1189|Q19735|WD=Q19735}}) Kasugasha Ryo Tarumi Nishimaki Enokizaka Goda Hatatoricho, {{ill|lt=Sadaharu|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153}}5 ({{ill|lt=1366|Q6194|WD=Q6194}})'s ``Kasugasha Ryō Tarumi Nishimaki Enokizakagouchi Toho Catalog'' also mentions Tarumi Shrine {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The changes during the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554}} period are not clear because there are almost no descriptions in historical materials{{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. According to the shrine's legend, it declined due to military fire {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, {{ill|lt=Tenwa (Japan)|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenwa}} In the 3rd year ({{ill|lt=1683|Q7651|WD=Q7651}}), the shrine building was rebuilt.After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), {{ill|lt=Modern company rating system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} It was listed as a {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}, and in February 1907 (Meiji 40|1907|Q2048}}, it was designated as a Shinto shrine). Later, in {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}49 ({{ill|lt=1974|Q2478|WD=Q2478}}), the shrine building you see today was rebuilt. === Modern === {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}}In April 2013, a briefing session was held for nearby residents about the plan to demolish Tarumi Shrine's "Chinju no Mori" to develop condominiums.Yomiuri Shimbun (Osaka Morning Edition), December 18, 2013, p. . Parishioners who learned about the condominium development at an information session formed the Tarumi Forest Protection Association and began opposing the project. Around 100 banners were erected around the shrine protesting against the construction of condominiums, posters opposing the construction were put up, and approximately 13,000 people submitted signatures opposing Suita City, which had granted the development permit. They also discovered that construction of condominiums would require roads on land owned by a shrine, and refused construction vehicles access, forcing the development to be halted. Asahi Shimbun (Osaka morning edition), January 11, 2014, p. 33. . {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} In February 2015, a condominium developer approached the shrine about purchasing the planned land, and in November of the same year, the shrine collected donations and purchased the land, and a contract was concluded, thus preserving the ``Chinju-no-Mori.'' According to Itoya, director of the NPO Shaso Gakkai, even if compromises such as revising plans are sometimes passed, it is rare for condominium development to stop. . === God level === * {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa}} 8th year ({{ill|lt=841|Q24870|WD=Q24870}}) Leap September 2nd, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Rank, Eighth Grade ("{{ill|lt=Shoku Nippon Koki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}") - Written as "Tarumi no Kami" Article on the 2nd day of Leap September of the 8th year of the Jowa era (841) in "Shoku Nihon Koki" (Refer to {{Harvnb|Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection}}). . * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} January 27, 3rd year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), from the upper fifth rank to the lower junior fourth rank ("{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}") - Written as "Tarumi no Kami" New Year's Day 27th, 1st year of Jogan (859) in "Nihon Sandai Jiroku" (Refer to {{Harvnb|Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection}}). . === Precincts === File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), shaden.jpg|Shrine building

Deities enshrined: Kanayamahiko no Mikoto, Kanayamahime no Mikoto, Toyoukehime no Mikoto, Kotosushi no Mikoto ** Haredo Shrine - Deities enshrined: Ibukito no Mikoto, Seoritsu Hime no Mikoto, Hasakai Tsu Hime no Mikoto, Hasasa Sura Hime no Mikoto ** Kotaisha - Deities enshrined: Isanaki no Mikoto, Isanami no Mikoto, Amaterasu Omikami ** Kusunosha Shrine - Deities enshrined: Owatatsumi no Mikoto, Ichikishimahimekami * Otabisho ** Kusunoki Shrine - Enshrined deity: Owatatsumi no Mikoto, Ichikishimahime no Kami * Others ** Fudosha File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Tarumi-jinja (Suita), keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinuchisha === Festival === The following festivals are held at Tarumi Shrine each year. * Monthly Festival/Monthly Festival (1st and 15th of every month) * Fudosha Goma (8th of every month) * Saitan Festival (January 1st) * Tarumi Ebisu (January 10th) * Donto Festival (January 15th) *Setsubun Festival (February 3rd) * Yoimiya (Otabisho Festival) (May 20th) * Spring Festival (May 21st) * Summer Purification (June 30th) * Yoimiya (Otabisho Festival) (October 20th) * Autumn Festival (October 21st) - It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchi|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. Currently {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Independent Shrine|UNKNOWN}} which does not belong to {{ill|lt=Shinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077}}Osaka Prefectural Shrine and Shrine Agency website [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai2shibu/suita-city/suita.html]. ===Enshrined God=== The following three deities are enshrined. . ; Main enshrined deity :* {{ill|lt=Toyoki Irihiko no Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375}} - 10th {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} prince. ; Aidenjin :* {{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Oonamuchi no Mikoto}} :* {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona no Mikoto}} In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}}, which was established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), there is one enshrined deity. In the special ``Engishiki'' festival, there will be one troupe for the prayer rain god festival and the Meishin festival, but there will be two za for the Yasojima festival. === About the enshrined deity === ``Tarumi'' in the company name of Tarumi Shrine means ``water flowing down a cliff'' {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1977}}. {{ill|lt=Senri Hills|Q11406845|WD=Q11406845}}The precincts located at the southern end are topographically located where spring water comes from the hills, and Tarumi-jin, who is enshrined here, has been worshiped as the god of water since ancient times {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikiuchi-sha)|1977}}. As mentioned below, in the literature, ``{{ill|lt=Newly Selected Names and Names Roku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}'' records the history of our company related to the water of ``Tarumi Oka'', and also {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Kokokushi}} records that the imperial court made offerings to the shrine many times to pray for rain {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1977}}. {{ill|lt=Early Modern Period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}}'s ``{{ill|lt=Settsu Meisho Zue|Q11498549|WD=Q11498549}}'' and ``Settsushi'' also contain descriptions of the spring water in this area, and in particular, ``Settsu Famous Places Zue'' describes how it was worshiped as a spiritual spring {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}}.In addition, in the Tarumi area, the ``Tarumi clan'' (Tarumi Uji, Tarumi ko) lived as an ancient clan, and it is known that they were involved in the rituals at Tarumi Shrine. Regarding this Tarumi clan, "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" by Ukyo Emperor In Tarumi Kojo, he was named as a descendant of Kamo no Shinchi-no-Mikoto (the fourth-generation grandchild of Toyoki Irihiko-no-Mikoto), and in his dispensation, he was given the surname of ``Tarumi-ko'' at the time of Arima no Kimi (the sixth-generation grandson of Toyoki Irihiko-no-Mikoto), and the festival was held at Tarumi Shrine. He is said to have been in charge of the shrine. Toyoki Irihiko-no-Mikoto, who is considered a personal deity at the current Tarumi Shrine, is the distant ancestor of the Tarumi clan. One theory is that before the involvement of the Tarumi clan, the shrine had been enshrined as a simple shrine dedicated to the god of water, but it is also speculated that due to the involvement of the Tarumi clan, Toyogi Irihiko no Mikoto was appointed as the personal deity {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. In addition, in the "Newly Selected Names and Names and Records", there is also a record of the history of Tarumi whose ancestor was Irihiko Toyoshiro no Mikoto by Sakyo-no-no-no-Ko.{{Sfn|Tarumi clan (Ancient clan names dictionary)|2010}}{{Sfn|Tarumi (Ancient clan dictionary)|2015}}.In the early modern "Settsu Famous Places Zue," the deity enshrined at the time was Toyogi Irihiko-no-Mikoto, and another theory states that the deity enshrined was {{ill|lt=Hitachikuni|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353}} Yakushi Shrine (currently {{ill|lt=Oarai Isosaki Shrine)|Q11437230|WD=Q11437230}}), and there is also a theory that they are Ōki Takamiko and Sukunahikona no Mikoto {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}, and the current Tarumi Shrine enshrines all three of these deities. Also, {{ill|lt=Nobutomo Ban|Q4346747|WD=Q4346747}} writes in ``{{ill|lt=Nihonshoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'' in ``{{ill|lt=Shinmeichō Kōshōdoshiro|Q11588823|WD=Q11588823}}''. {jalink|Empress Jingu|Q232803}} The theory is that the enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Yoriami Otarimi|Q24899491|WD=Q24899491}}, which appears in the {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1977}}. === History === === Founding === The date of its construction is unknown {{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1977}}. "{{ill|lt=Newly selected family name list|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}" Ukyo no Betsu According to Tarumi Kojo, the time of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kotoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980}} ({{ill|lt=645 years|Q31040|WD=Q31040}}-{{jalink |654|Q31109}}), there was a drought, but Prince Arima (grandchild of Toyogi Irihiko no Mikoto VI) traveled from Tarumi-oka to Takahi to visit the Emperor's Palace ({{ill|lt=difficult). It is said that because of his accomplishments, Prince Arima was given the surname ``Ko Tarumi'' and was put in charge of Tarumi Shrine. Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|WD=Tarumi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|lt=1977}}{{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}{{Sfn|Tarumi Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. It is located in the center of the Tarumi ruins and Tarumi Minami ruins, which are thought to be the core settlements of the Yayoi period. === Outline === According to the ``{{ill|lt=Shinshōkakuchofusho|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312}}'' {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}), the ``Sumiyoshi Tarumijin'' at that time was {{ill|lt=Kobe (Private)|Q11589711|WD=Q11589711|lt=Kobe (Kanbe)}} 22 houses are allocated, of which 2 houses are {{ill|lt=Tenpyoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307}}3 years ({{ill|lt=759 years|Q30532|WD=Q30532}}) December 29th mark. From nk|Settsu Province|Q864163}}, one house was filled from {{ill|lt=Bicchu|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514}} with the September 7th mark of the first year of {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181|WD=Q1373181}} ({{ill|lt=765|Q31216|WD=Q31216}}). Since the name of the god is prefixed with "Sumiyoshi" in this entry, there is a theory that suggests that it was closely related to {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949}} around this time. .
